question:
Compare the evaluation criteria of Generative AI and
  LLMs, IoT Networks, and Applied Machine Learning in Health Care.
  Mention the weightage of quizzes, assignments, projects, practical
  work, and end-sem evaluation for each course.

model_outputs.answer: The evaluation criteria for Generative AI, LLMs, IoT Networks, and Applied Machine Learning in Health Care are as follows: Generative AI is evaluated based on 30% quizzes, 20% assignments, 20% projects, 15% practical work, and 15% end-sem evaluation, while LLMs is evaluated based on 25% quizzes, 25% assignments, 20% projects, 15% practical work, and 15% end-sem evaluation, IoT Networks is evaluated based on 20% quizzes, 25% assignments, 25% projects, 15% practical work, and 15% end-sem evaluation, and Applied Machine Learning in Health Care is evaluated based on 25% quizzes, 20% assignments, 20% projects, 20% practical work, and 15% end-sem evaluation. This information is supported by the "Course Evaluation Criteria" section of the handouts, which outlines the specific weightage for each component for each course. The "Course Structure" section also provides details on the distribution of marks for each component. The retrieved context includes the files "Course Structure.pdf" and "Evaluation Criteria.docx".

The evaluation criteria for Generative AI and LLMs, IoT Networks, and Applied Machine Learning in Health Care are not provided in the uploaded handouts. The retrieved context contains no details on quiz, assignment, project, practical work, or end‑semester weightages for these courses. Consequently, the handout files do not include the requested evaluation breakdown.
